name: Ch'ueh Ch'ao Chi Ting (Stir-fried Chicken In
recipeInstructions:
1. Dice the chicken into 1/2 inch pieces and marinate for half an hour with the marinating sauce (1 tbsp egg white, 1 1/2 tbsp cornstarch, and 1 tbsp soysauce). Fry in lukewarm oil until the meat becomes white, then drain.
2. Heat a pan with 3 tbsp oil and saute scallion, ginger, mushrooms, water chestnuts, carrot, and green pepper. Add the chicken cubes and seasonings. Increase heat to high, stir, and pour into bird's nest.
3. Bird's Nest: Cut potatoes into thin shreds, rinse in cold water, drain, and dry. Mix with 1/3 tsp salt and 1 cup cornstarch in a bowl. Place potato shreds through one strainer and press through another strainer submerged in very hot oil. Deep fry for about 4 minutes until golden brown. Drain and remove from the strainer.
4. Shred green vegetables or shred lettuce and place under the nest for decoration.
Note: This recipe is adapted from Pei Mei's Chinese Cookbook Volume II by Fu, printed by Chiu-Yu Printing Co, Ltd in 1974.
